objectives.text: Students will identify and name at least 8 different living things (plants and animals) in their local environment with 80% accuracy
objectives.standard: NGSS.K-LS1-1: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants and animals need to survive
objectives.assessment_method: Nature walk observation checklist with teacher-led questioning

objectives.text: Students will explain that living things grow, move, need food, and respond to their environment by sorting picture cards into 'living' vs 'non-living' categories with 90% accuracy
objectives.standard: NGSS.K-LS1-1: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants and animals need to survive
objectives.assessment_method: Sorting activity worksheet with visual criteria checklist

objectives.text: Students will describe the basic needs of plants (water, sunlight, soil) by labeling a diagram and predicting what happens when one need is missing
objectives.standard: NGSS.1-LS1-1: Use materials to design a solution to a human problem by mimicking plants and animals
objectives.assessment_method: Labeled diagram assessment and 'what if' prediction prompt

objectives.text: Students will compare how different animals move (walk, fly, swim, crawl) by matching animals to their movement type with 85% accuracy
objectives.standard: NGSS.1-LS1-1: Use materials to design a solution to a human problem by mimicking plants and animals
objectives.assessment_method: Matching quiz with visual support and teacher observation
